event: presented with painful right scrotal swelling
scrotal swelling increased gradually over 2 days
white blood cells count elevated (20000 cells per microliter)
ultrasound of the scrotum showed normal testis
thick fluid collection around the testis
diagnosis of infective process most likely
abdominal X-ray erect position showed mild bilateral pleural effusions
dilated loops of the small bowel
abdominal ultrasound showed collection of fluid at the pelvic cavity
CT scan abdomen showed large amount of air collection in retroperitoneal space
retroperitoneal air displacing right kidney toward midline
retroperitoneal air extending to right scrotum
CT scan with oral contrast showed tracking of contrast media to retroperitoneum
contrast media extending to right scrotum
large amount of thin pus in peritoneal cavity
air and fluid collection in retroperitoneal space
intraoperative evaluation of small bowel showed no abnormality
intraoperative evaluation of large bowel showed no abnormality
perforation in posterior wall of duodenum
suturing with slowly absorbable suture material
peritoneal cavity washed with warm normal saline
